subject: Fix Retries for Policies
commit_message:
Fix Retries for Policies

This applies changes to make retries work properly
for all control loops. The current design was ignoring
the upper bound of the retries and retrying until either
a success or control loop timeout occured. This is now
fixed to only do retries until the limit is reached that
is specified from the policy.

The operation is now started in the GUARD.PERMITTED rule.
I think this is better because it stops Policy from doing
extra processing if there is a guard deny. This is also
needed so that we can properly do retries for all cases.

The notifications sent in GUARD_NOT_YET_QUERIED and
GUARD.RESPONSE are now more informative with the
message specifying the actor and recipe. The not queried
rule has a message stating that we are sending a query
to guard and the guard response message in the guard
response rule specifices the result from guard.

During a retest of vDNS it appeared that the archetype
template was no longer working, this was because there
were changes in the JUnit template that were not
reflected in the archetype template. These were added
to archetype and vDNS is verified to work again.

Another small fix needed was making sure the action for
vCPE is "Restart" instead of "restart". APPC will
reject our request if "Restart" is not sent as the
action.

Issue-ID: POLICY-259
Change-Id: I28dd3c9a629d297b408775a01afadd5c19351e37
Signed-off-by: Daniel Cruz <dc443y@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/333/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/333

Patch Set 1:

Michael, Chenfei, can we get fast resolution on this? it is critical this gets submitted soon due to our coding deadline.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Michael is in PTO today and tomorrow. My only concern is if there is a guard deny and another operational policy chained to failure_guard, can it be proceeded? I roughly remember that was the reason why we should startOperation before querying guard. Otherwise, if we get a guard deny, finishOperation will not really work since there is no currentOperation assigned before. Thus, it will just throw an exception saying no operation to finish and not proceed. Daniel, could you check that?

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/334/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/334

Patch Set 2:

> Michael is in PTO today and tomorrow. My only concern is if there
 > is a guard deny and another operational policy chained to
 > failure_guard, can it be proceeded? I roughly remember that was the
 > reason why we should startOperation before querying guard.
 > Otherwise, if we get a guard deny, finishOperation will not really
 > work since there is no currentOperation assigned before. Thus, it
 > will just throw an exception saying no operation to finish and not
 > proceed. Daniel, could you check that?

I see what you are saying. I agree this should be fixed but just an FYI for myself, is Policy chaining going to be used in R1?

Patch Set 2: -Code-Review

Policy chaining is supported in platform capabilities. Current yaml specification, drools template actually support it. User has flexibility to specify operational policy chaining from CLAMP GUI and push down to policy yaml which is enforced by drools rules. Although we already support it, in R1, we don't have use case to demonstrate it. We just need one operational policy in each of R1 use cases. Just keep in mind that it is fine for R1 use cases but it will result in a bug if user wants to chain subsequent policy to failure_guard.

Patch Set 2:

Going to merge this.   We'll defer work policy chaining work to R2
Change has been successfully merged by Jorge Hernandez
